domain: Brood-X 17-year periodical cicada (prime-numbered emergence period coprime with shorter predator cycles)
specific_mechanism: Prime-numbered life-cycle period (13, 17) ensures the brood's emergence is coprime with predator cycles, so any short-cycle predator (period 2-6) coincides with cicada emergence rarely — only every LCM = 13/17 × predator_cycle years.
llm_application: PRIME-CYCLE-REPLAY: continual-learning replay-buffer refresh + curriculum task rotation triggered at PRIME-numbered epoch periods (e.g., 13, 17, 19, 23) explicitly chosen coprime with other periodic training schedules (lr cosine period, eval cadence, weight-decay cycle, gradient-norm-clip cadence). De-aligns periodic schedules so that gradient interference does not resonance-amplify catastrophic forgetting.
novelty_claim: Standard replay (uniform k-epoch), surprise-driven (SURE), Ebbinghaus expanding-interval, MSSR memory-aware all use period chosen by surprise/utility metric; none deliberately select PRIME-numbered periods coprime with concurrent periodic schedules to avoid resonance-driven gradient interference. Coprime-cycle de-alignment is mathematically distinct from adaptive scheduling.
